domain: Welsh eisteddfod bardic chair tradition (Welsh poetry festival since 1176; awdl in strict cynghanedd 24 metres codified 1523; bardic chair awarded annually to winning Prifardd; chair physically passes; winner-lineage tracked across centuries)
specific_mechanism: EISTEDDFOD-CHAIR-CASCADE: tournament-cascade LLM generation with strict-meter constraint + chair-lineage chain-of-winners propagation. (1) Generate N candidate outputs each under strict-meter constraint (24-rule formal grammar). (2) LLM-judge selects winning candidate as 'bardic-chair winner' for round t. (3) Subsequent generations (t+1, t+2, ...) inherit winner's constraint-satisfaction pattern via in-context-example chain {w_1, w_2, ..., w_{t-1}}. (4) Lineage fine-tune: optionally fine-tune small adapter on winning outputs accumulated across rounds (chair lineage = fine-tune accumulator). (5) Differs from plain best-of-N by chain-of-winners constraint-propagation through ICL or adapter; differs from chain-of-thought by tournament-judged-winner selection per step.
llm_application: EISTEDDFOD-CHAIR-CASCADE tournament-cascade strict-meter best-of-N constrained LLM generation + chair-lineage chain-of-winners ICL/adapter propagation + 24-metre formal-rule constraint. Differs from R413 CHASQUI-RELAY (relay). R422 + R429 KAMI-SHIBAI. R438 PIROGUE-CASCADE. R455 HAUSA-ZANGO. R403 WELSH-PENILLION (penillion solo). R480 RINGDANS-CASCADE (chorus consensus). Differs from Constrained Decoding 2403.06988 (single-pass formal-language enforcement — closest, but no tournament + lineage). CRANE 2502.09061 (reasoning + constraint). PoeTone 2508.02515 (Songci constrained — closest poetry-LLM, no tournament-lineage). Awesome LLM Constrained Decoding. Differs by TOURNAMENT BEST-OF-N + CHAIN-OF-WINNERS LINEAGE + CHAIR-FINE-TUNE ACCUMULATOR.
novelty_claim: Constrained Decoding + CRANE + PoeTone cover constrained-generation paradigm. EISTEDDFOD-CHAIR-CASCADE adds (a) tournament best-of-N + (b) chain-of-winners ICL/adapter inheritance + (c) chair-fine-tune lineage accumulator. Recombination — borderline lookup.
